Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has announced that the Texas Business Court granted his motion for expedited discovery, requiring Fermi to provide documentation and testimony regarding its recent actions, including the imposition of a 70% supermajority voting requirement. This development is a significant win for Neugebauer and Fermi's shareholders, who have been seeking greater accountability and transparency from the company's board. Neugebauer will also host a Town Hall webinar with analysts on June 30 to address questions and provide updates on his efforts to restore "good governance" to Fermi.

Neugebauer, co-founder and largest individual shareholder of Fermi America, Inc., has proposed a town hall webinar for June 30th where he, Governor Rick Perry, and other parties will answer questions from analysts and journalists about the company's past, present, and future. Neugebauer is seeking to give shareholders a say in the company's direction, particularly regarding Project Matador, a 7,500-acre private power campus in Texas, and whether to pursue a strategic dual-track process to maximize shareholder value. The proposed webinar comes after Governor Perry and the board gave themselves a 70% supermajority vote threshold, making it difficult for shareholders to change course.

Neugebauer, along with other participants, has issued a press release reaffirming his support for John Sellers of Double Eagle as Chairman and CEO of Fermi, citing the need for enhanced accountability and credibility to prevent value destruction by the current Board and management team. Neugebauer claims that the current Board has failed to replace nine key senior executives who departed Fermi after his termination and has ignored proposals from large companies that could offer greater value to shareholders.
